executive_summary: A venture-AGNOSTIC customer-support intake->triage->route pipeline that handles the happy path with ZERO operator-in-loop, flipping KR-2026-07-01 (0->1: 'Automated support intake/triage pipeline live end-to-end'). Three composable seams: (1) channel-neutral intake ingestion normalizes any raw support input into a support-ticket object; (2) a triage classifier REUSES the EVA intake-classification keyword-scoring engine pattern (lib/integrations/intake-classifier.js keywordClassify) to assign category + severity + a routing decision (NOT a greenfield classifier); (3) routing/disposition AUTO-RESOLVES the happy path and ESCALATES edge cases to a SURFACED queue (fail-loud, never silent-drop). Persistence REUSES the existing surfaced feedback store (no new table / no migration): escalations land as feedback rows (category='support_escalation', visible via /inbox); the pipeline core is pure/composable so it is unit-testable without a DB. On a successful end-to-end run over a seeded happy-path ticket, the pipeline flips KR-2026-07-01 current_value 0->1. Out of scope: any venture-specific support content/desk, human staffing, live-chat UI, SLA dashboards, and the >90% zero-touch rate (the venture pick is HELD).

functional_requirements.title: Channel-neutral intake ingestion seam
functional_requirements.description: lib/support/intake-pipeline.js normalizeSupportTicket(raw) -> a normalized support-ticket object { ticket_id, channel, subject, body, customer_ref, received_at, status:'new' } from any channel-neutral raw input (email/web/api shape). Pure + tolerant (missing fields degrade gracefully, never throws).
acceptance_criteria: a raw input from any channel normalizes to a ticket with the required fields; a deterministic ticket_id is derived when absent
missing/empty fields are tolerated (no throw); channel defaults to 'unknown'
the function is pure (no DB) and unit-tested

functional_requirements.title: Triage classifier (REUSE the EVA intake-classifier pattern, no greenfield)
functional_requirements.description: triageSupportTicket(ticket) -> { category, severity, routing_decision, confidence, rationale }. The keyword-scoring is MODELLED ON / imports the pattern from lib/integrations/intake-classifier.js keywordClassify (same scoring shape) adapted to a support taxonomy: category in {billing, bug, how_to, account, abuse, other}; severity in {low, medium, high}; routing_decision in {auto_resolve, escalate}. High severity OR category abuse OR unknown/other category OR low confidence => escalate. Pure/fail-open per ticket (an error degrades to escalate, never throws).
acceptance_criteria: category + severity + routing_decision are assigned deterministically via keyword-scoring (reuses the intake-classifier pattern; no duplicate greenfield classifier)
a clear happy-path ticket (known category, low/med severity, confident) => routing_decision='auto_resolve'
high-severity / abuse / ambiguous / unknown-category => routing_decision='escalate' (fail-loud default)
evaluator is pure and never throws (error => escalate)

functional_requirements.title: Routing/disposition + surfaced escalation (reuse the feedback store)
functional_requirements.description: disposeSupportTicket(supabase, ticket, triage): when routing_decision='auto_resolve' record an auto-resolution (status='auto_resolved', a canned per-category disposition) ; when 'escalate' write the ticket to the EXISTING surfaced feedback store (category='support_escalation', status 'new') so it appears in /inbox. NO new table / NO migration. NEVER silent-drop: anything not confidently auto-resolvable is escalated. Fail-soft DB layer (a write error is logged + still returns the disposition, and an auto_resolve that fails to record DOWNGRADES to escalate so nothing is lost).
acceptance_criteria: happy-path ticket => disposition.status='auto_resolved' with a category-appropriate canned resolution
edge-case ticket => a feedback row (category='support_escalation') is written and is queryable (surfaced) — never dropped
no new table or migration is introduced (reuses feedback)
a DB write failure never silently drops a ticket (downgrade-to-escalate / fail-loud)

functional_requirements.title: Pipeline orchestrator + escalation-queue surface
functional_requirements.description: runSupportPipeline(supabase, raw) chains normalizeSupportTicket -> triageSupportTicket -> disposeSupportTicket and returns { ticket, triage, disposition }. getSupportEscalationQueue(supabase, opts) lists the surfaced escalations (feedback where category='support_escalation'). Plus a seed/demo entrypoint (scripts/support-intake-demo.cjs or npm script) that runs a seeded happy-path ticket end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: runSupportPipeline returns the end-to-end result (ticket+triage+disposition) for a happy-path AND an edge-case input
getSupportEscalationQueue returns the escalated tickets (the surfaced queue)
a seeded happy-path ticket flows end-to-end with NO operator action

functional_requirements.title: Wire KR-2026-07-01 (flip 0->1 on a real end-to-end run)
functional_requirements.description: On a successful end-to-end pipeline run (intake->triage->routed) over a seeded happy-path ticket, flip key_results code='KR-2026-07-01' current_value 0->1 and status pending->achieved. IDEMPOTENT (re-running does not regress/inflate) and only fires on a genuine end-to-end run (not on a partial/errored run). Update by code; no schema change.
acceptance_criteria: after the seeded happy-path demo, key_results.current_value for code='KR-2026-07-01' = 1 (status achieved)
the flip is idempotent (re-run leaves it at 1, not 2)
a partial/errored run does NOT flip the KR

functional_requirements.title: Tests + seeded demo + venture-agnostic / reuse guards
functional_requirements.description: Unit tests: normalizeSupportTicket (happy + missing fields), triageSupportTicket (auto_resolve happy path + escalate on high-severity/abuse/ambiguous), disposeSupportTicket (auto_resolved + escalation-written via injected supabase mock). Integration test: runSupportPipeline end-to-end (happy-path auto_resolve + edge-case escalation surfaced) with an injected store. A guard test asserting the triage classifier REUSES the intake-classifier keyword-scoring pattern (single classifier, no duplicate greenfield) and that the pipeline carries NO venture-specific content/branching.
acceptance_criteria: per-seam unit tests (pass + edge) green
integration test: happy-path auto_resolve + edge-case escalation (surfaced) + KR-flip (mocked) green
reuse guard: no duplicate greenfield classifier; venture-agnostic (no venture-specific branch)

technical_requirements.title: Reuse the EVA intake-classifier pattern
technical_requirements.description: The triage classifier reuses lib/integrations/intake-classifier.js keywordClassify scoring shape (imported or mirrored as a single canonical support classifier) adapted to the support taxonomy. Do NOT greenfield a parallel classifier engine.

technical_requirements.title: Venture-agnostic, channel-neutral
technical_requirements.description: No venture-specific support content, branching, or first-venture-offer content (the pick is HELD). Intake is channel-neutral (the capture seam is decoupled from any specific channel).

technical_requirements.title: Reuse the feedback store; no new table / no migration
technical_requirements.description: The surfaced escalation queue REUSES the existing feedback table (category='support_escalation', surfaced via /inbox). No new table, no schema migration, no RLS change. The pipeline core is pure (DB injected) so it is testable without a DB. No silent-drop.

technical_requirements.title: KR flip is idempotent + real-run-gated
technical_requirements.description: Flip key_results by code='KR-2026-07-01' (current_value 0->1, status->achieved) ONLY on a genuine end-to-end run; idempotent on re-run; no schema change.

technical_requirements.title: Out of scope
technical_requirements.description: Does NOT build a venture-specific support desk, first-venture support content, human-agent staffing, live-chat UI, or SLA dashboards; does NOT cover the >90% zero-touch RATE (this SD delivers the happy-path pipeline + KR-flip).

test_scenarios.expected: disposition.status='auto_resolved', no escalation feedback row, KR-flip fires (mocked)

test_scenarios.scenario: high-severity 'service is down, charged twice' ticket -> escalate
test_scenarios.expected: routing_decision='escalate', a feedback row (category support_escalation) written + surfaced in the queue
re-running the end-to-end pipeline leaves KR-2026-07-01 current_value=1 (not 2)
single canonical support classifier reusing the intake-classifier pattern; no venture-specific branch

acceptance_criteria: A venture-agnostic intake->triage->route pipeline runs the happy path zero-touch (seeded ticket auto-resolved with no operator action) and flips KR-2026-07-01 0->1 (idempotent, real-run-gated)
Edge cases (high-severity / abuse / ambiguous / unknown) escalate to a SURFACED queue (existing feedback store, category='support_escalation') — never silent-dropped; the queue is queryable

The triage classifier REUSES the EVA intake-classifier keyword-scoring pattern (no duplicate greenfield); no new table / no migration; unit + integration tests green; adversarial review of fail-loud/no-silent-drop + reuse + KR-idempotency clean

risks.risk: Greenfielding a classifier instead of reusing the EVA engine
risks.mitigation: triage classifier mirrors/imports intake-classifier.js keywordClassify; a guard test asserts single-classifier reuse
fail-loud default-to-escalate; auto_resolve that fails to record downgrades to escalate; integration test pins escalation surfacing

risks.risk: KR double-flip / flip on a partial run
risks.mitigation: idempotent update by code + real-end-to-end-run gate; unit test pins idempotency

risks.risk: Scope creep into venture-specific support / staffing / SLA dashboards
risks.mitigation: TR-5 out-of-scope boundary; venture-agnostic guard test

system_architecture.summary: One pure pipeline module (lib/support/intake-pipeline.js) with three composable seams (normalize/triage/dispose) + an orchestrator + a queue surface, reusing the intake-classifier keyword-scoring pattern for triage and the existing feedback store for surfaced escalations, with a KR-flip on a real end-to-end run. No new table; DB is injected so the core is testable without it.

components.name: lib/support/intake-pipeline.js
components.change: NEW: normalizeSupportTicket, triageSupportTicket (reuse intake-classifier pattern), disposeSupportTicket, runSupportPipeline, getSupportEscalationQueue, flipPipelineLiveKR (FR-1..FR-5)

components.name: lib/integrations/intake-classifier.js
components.change: REUSE: keywordClassify scoring pattern (imported/mirrored; not modified)

components.name: feedback table (existing)
components.change: REUSE: escalations written as category='support_escalation' (surfaced via /inbox); no schema change

components.name: key_results table (existing)
components.change: UPDATE: code='KR-2026-07-01' current_value 0->1 + status achieved on a real end-to-end run (no schema change)

components.name: scripts/support-intake-demo.cjs
components.change: NEW: seeded happy-path end-to-end demo entrypoint (flips the KR)

implementation_approach.summary: Author a pure, composable support pipeline (normalize->triage->dispose) reusing the intake-classifier keyword-scoring pattern for triage; escalate edge cases to the existing surfaced feedback store (no new table); auto-resolve the happy path; flip KR-2026-07-01 on a real end-to-end run (idempotent); pin with per-seam unit + an end-to-end integration test (injected store) + reuse/venture-agnostic guards; adversarially review fail-loud/no-silent-drop + reuse + KR-idempotency before merge.
steps: triage classifier reuses intake-classifier keyword-scoring (support taxonomy: category+severity+routing)
escalations -> feedback (category support_escalation, surfaced); auto-resolve happy path; fail-loud no-silent-drop
KR-2026-07-01 flip 0->1 idempotent on real end-to-end run; seeded demo entrypoint
PRD -> adversarial review -> PR -> full chain -> LFA

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Run the seeded happy-path demo (node scripts/support-intake-demo.cjs or the npm script) which sends a clear billing/how-to ticket through runSupportPipeline
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: the ticket is normalized, triaged (category+severity), AUTO-RESOLVED with no operator action (disposition.status='auto_resolved'), and KR-2026-07-01 current_value flips 0->1 (status achieved)

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Send an edge-case ticket (high-severity 'service down, double-charged' / abuse / ambiguous) through runSupportPipeline and query getSupportEscalationQueue
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: routing_decision='escalate'; a feedback row (category='support_escalation') is written and appears in the surfaced escalation queue — the ticket is NEVER silently dropped

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Re-run the happy-path demo and re-read KR-2026-07-01; confirm the triage classifier reuses the intake-classifier pattern (single classifier, no greenfield duplicate)
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: KR-2026-07-01 stays at current_value=1 (idempotent, not 2); exactly one support triage classifier exists, reusing lib/integrations/intake-classifier.js keywordClassify pattern
